name: E03S03 — Tech Notification SMS with Magic Link

nodes.name: Trigger: Tech SMS Webhook
nodes.notes:
Triggered after E03S02 stores the magic link token.
Expects POST body: { tech_id, job_id, token, client_id } — all UUID strings.

INTEGRATION: In the E03S02 workflow, add an HTTP Request node on the true branch
of 'Token PATCH Succeeded?' that POSTs to:
  POST https://nexusfound.cloud/webhook/sendjob-tech-sms
Body: {
  'tech_id': {{ $('Generate Magic Link Token').item.json.tech_id }},
  'job_id': {{ $('Trigger: Job Assigned Webhook').item.json.body.job_id }},
  'token': {{ $('Generate Magic Link Token').item.json.token }},
  'client_id': {{ $('Trigger: Job Assigned Webhook').item.json.body.client_id }}
}
(Use actual E03S02 node names from your imported workflow.)

nodes.name: GET Job Details
nodes.notes:
AC2: Fetches customer_name, customer_phone, service_address, issue_description, client_id from jobs table.
Filtered by job_id from the webhook payload.
Supabase HTTP Request pattern — NOT native Supabase node (DEC-003).

IMPORTANT: Replace SUPABASE_ANON_KEY_PLACEHOLDER with your anon key from:
  Supabase Dashboard → Project Settings → API → anon public
RLS SELECT policy on jobs table must allow anon reads.

Reference: $('GET Job Details').item.json.customer_name

nodes.name: GET Client Details
nodes.notes:
AC5: Fetches business_name and twilio_number from clients table.
business_name is NEVER hardcoded — always pulled from Supabase (AC5).
twilio_number is the client's 10DLC Twilio number used as SMS 'from'.

Reference: $('GET Client Details').item.json.business_name

nodes.name: GET Tech Details
nodes.notes:
AC6: Fetches contact_method to determine SMS vs email path.
Also fetches phone (SMS 'to'), magic_link_token (confirmation), and name (for logging).
RLS SELECT policy on techs table must allow anon reads.

Reference: $('GET Tech Details').item.json.contact_method

nodes.name: Build SMS Body
nodes.notes:
AC2: Assembles all 6 required data points: business_name, customer_name, service_address, customer_phone, issue_description, magic_link_url.
AC3: Magic link URL = 'https://tech.sendjob.app?token=[token]'
AC4: Uses canonical template exactly as defined in conventions.md.
AC5: business_name read from $('GET Client Details').item.json.business_name — never hardcoded.
Opt-out: 'Reply STOP to unsubscribe.' appended per Twilio A2P 10DLC compliance requirements.

IMPORTANT: No require('crypto') used — token is passed in from webhook payload (generated by E03S02).
All node references use $('Node Name').item.json.field pattern (n8n 2.x).

nodes.name: Contact Method: SMS or Email?
nodes.notes:
AC6: Routes to SMS path (true) or email stub path (false) based on techs.contact_method.
True: contact_method = 'sms' → Send Tech SMS node
False: contact_method = 'email' (or any other value) → Log Email Stub node

SMS is the primary path for Sprint 3. Email path is an acceptable stub per AC6.

nodes.name: Send Tech SMS
nodes.notes:
AC1, AC2, AC7: Sends the tech notification SMS via native n8n Twilio node.
AC7: Uses native Twilio node — NOT HTTP Request (per conventions.md, DEC).
from: clients.twilio_number (the client's 10DLC number)
to: techs.phone (the assigned tech's phone — E.164 format)
message: canonical SMS body assembled in 'Build SMS Body' node

CREDENTIAL: Twilio (Account SID + Auth Token)
Configure in n8n: Settings → Credentials → New → Twilio → enter Account SID + Auth Token

nodes.name: Log: Email Stub
nodes.notes:
AC6: Email stub — logs that tech has contact_method='email' and notification was not sent.
This is acceptable for Sprint 3 per AC6 ('Email path is acceptable as a stub').
Implement actual Resend email in a future sprint.
Workflow does NOT error out — it continues to respond cleanly to the caller.

nodes.name: Respond to Webhook
nodes.notes:
Returns 200 OK to the E03S02 caller after SMS send (or email stub).
Both SMS and email stub paths merge into this node.

_comment:
INTEGRATION NOTES — READ BEFORE IMPORTING

== SUPABASE KEY ==
Replace all 'SUPABASE_ANON_KEY_PLACEHOLDER' values with your actual anon key.
Location: Supabase Dashboard → Project Settings → API → 'anon public'
Required in: GET Job Details, GET Client Details, GET Tech Details (3 nodes)

Verify RLS policies:
  - jobs table: anon SELECT policy must exist
  - clients table: anon SELECT policy must exist  
  - techs table: anon SELECT policy must exist
See Supabase → Auth → Policies for each table.

== TWILIO CREDENTIALS ==
Configure Twilio credentials in n8n:
  n8n → Settings → Credentials → New → Twilio
  Enter: Account SID + Auth Token from Twilio Console
The 'Send Tech SMS' node uses credential id 'twilio-credentials' — update this to match
your n8n credential ID after creation.

== INTEGRATION WITH E03S02 ==
This workflow is triggered by E03S02 after the token is stored successfully.
In the E03S02 workflow, add an HTTP Request node on the TRUE branch of
'Token PATCH Succeeded?' (before 'Respond to Webhook'):

  Method: POST
  URL: https://nexusfound.cloud/webhook/sendjob-tech-sms
  Body (JSON):
  {
    "tech_id": "{{ $('Generate Magic Link Token').item.json.tech_id }}",
    "job_id": "{{ $('Trigger: Job Assigned Webhook').item.json.body.job_id }}",
    "token": "{{ $('Generate Magic Link Token').item.json.token }}",
    "client_id": "{{ $('Trigger: Job Assigned Webhook').item.json.body.client_id }}"
  }

IMPORTANT: Use the actual node names from your imported E03S02 workflow.
All expression fields require the orange '=' toggle to be active in n8n.

== FIELD REFERENCES (n8n 2.x — critical) ==
  ALWAYS: $('Node Name').item.json.fieldname
  NEVER:  $json.fieldname for upstream node data
  Code node output: return [{ json: { ... } }]
  Expression mode: toggle orange '=' icon per field

== WEBHOOK PATH ==
  POST https://nexusfound.cloud/webhook/sendjob-tech-sms

== TEST PROCEDURE ==
1. Import this workflow into n8n
2. Replace SUPABASE_ANON_KEY_PLACEHOLDER in all 3 HTTP Request nodes
3. Configure Twilio credentials (Account SID + Auth Token)
4. Activate the workflow
5. Send test POST to https://nexusfound.cloud/webhook/sendjob-tech-sms:
   { "tech_id": "<real_tech_uuid>", "job_id": "<real_job_uuid>", "token": "<test_token>", "client_id": "<real_client_uuid>" }
6. Verify SMS received on tech's phone with correct business name, job details, and magic link URL
7. Test on actual mobile device — field techs read these on phones, not desktops
